summary: Reverse shell to 185.112.147.174:7007 on module load
details: paperclip-adapter-helpers@1.0.10 contains an unconditional reverse-shell payload at the top level of dist/server/index.js, which is reached via the package's main entry that re-exports from./server. When the package is require()'d or import()'d, it spawns a detached `sh -c` running a Python/bash one-liner that connects stdio to 185.112.147.174:7007, giving the operator of that host interactive shell access on the installer's machine. The spawn is detached with stdio ignored and wrapped in a try/catch to hide failures. The package additionally impersonates the Paperclip adapter ecosystem (declared dep `@paperclipai/adapter-utils`; README instructs installing `vps-new-manager` via a Paperclip adapter API), indicating a namespace-abuse lure delivering the backdoor.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
